candidate_trials.candidate_summary: Measure the current Spark platform fit before adding Roblox-specific infrastructure.
candidate_trials.hypothesis: Spark already handles bounded planning and evidence routing, but Roblox execution remains largely unimplemented.

candidate_trials.candidate_summary: Build the smallest honest Roblox lane around an obby-style prototype with repo scaffold and bench evidence.
candidate_trials.hypothesis: An obby foundation lane is the fastest path to prove brief-to-project generation, Studio sync, and a playable loop.

candidate_trials.candidate_summary: Push from scaffold into a prototype loop by prioritizing Studio sync for the same low-complexity genre.
candidate_trials.hypothesis: After scaffolding, the next leverage is Studio-connected iteration on a small playable obby prototype.

candidate_trials.candidate_summary: Evaluate whether the system is ready for a deeper tycoon vertical slice with playtest telemetry.
candidate_trials.hypothesis: Tycoon scope becomes plausible only after the platform can observe session flow, retention hooks, and economy behavior.

candidate_trials.candidate_summary: Stress the missing launch and economy surfaces for a simulator-style game.
candidate_trials.hypothesis: Simulator launch prep should currently score as underbuilt because monetization, balancing, and release gates are not wired yet.

self_edit.prompt_preamble: Keep the Roblox chip honest about what is actually implemented. Prefer the smallest end-to-end game delivery lane over ambitious but ungrounded scope.
